title: Hemionitis newberryi
what_description: Hemionitis newberryi is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It is an evergreen, terrestrial fern with a rhizome that is covered with scales and produces long, slender stems. The leaves are pinnate, with 2-4 pairs of pinnules that are lance-shaped and have a pointed apex. The frond is 30-60 cm long and has a petiole that is 5-15 cm long. The sporangia are arranged in a sori along the veins of the pinnules, and the plant is native to the southeastern United States.

taxonomy: Hemionitis newberryi is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This fern is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is commonly found in arid regions. It is a member of the genus Hemionitis, which consists of species that are primarily found in North and South America. The Dryopteridaceae family is a large and diverse group of ferns that includes over 2,000 species.
